meta.description: An identifiable single emergency incident such as a fire, vehicle crash, explosion, flood, accident, or general emergency. Each incident is a specific occurrence distinguishable by its location and date — not a trend, overview, or general topic about emergencies. Extracted from news articles and social media.

example.description: Fire breaks out in a warehouse in the industrial zone, firefighters responded for 4 hours.
example.context: The warehouse stored chemical products. No injuries reported but nearby businesses were evacuated as a precaution.

date_range.mention: el incendio inicio a las 2:30 de la tarde y fue controlado 4 horas despues

example.damage_description: bodega con productos quimicos destruida, negocios cercanos evacuados

event_type.description: Type of emergency from the catalogue. Choose the single most specific category that matches.

name.description: Name or identifier for the emergency, if any. Usually null.

context.description: Contextual information: causes, related conditions, previous incidents.

relevance.description: Relevance within the article: 1 = main topic, 2 = secondary, 3 = implicit.

date_range.description: Date or date range of the emergency. Extract the start datetime and, if present, the end datetime.

casualties.description: Casualty count (dead, injured, missing), if mentioned.

damage_description.description: Description of material or property damage.

response_agencies.description: Agencies or services that responded (firefighters, civil protection, paramedics, etc.).

cause.description: Cause of the emergency, if mentioned or suspected.

vehicles_involved.description: Number of vehicles involved in traffic accidents/crashes, with original text mention and parsed count.

evacuations.description: Description of evacuations, if any occurred.
